title: Claude bridge: architectural cleanup (claude_bridge.rs)
analysis: Task 352 addresses 6 review suggestions from task 139 in the claude_bridge module. The changes are ordered to minimize churn: S5 (trivial ordering fix) → S4 (outbound_to_event return type) → S2 (state mutex type) → S1 (module boundary decoupling) → S3 (ready_notify restriction) → S8 (boilerplate extraction). Key interactions: S1 and S4 both change from_parts_with_mcp's generic bounds; S2 changes the state Mutex type used pervasively in tests and production code; S3 depends on having an alternative to the raw ready_notify accessor; S8 depends on S2 (uses lock().unwrap() in the extracted helper). The existing test file has ~60 tests covering IPC types, parse/format, read_sidecar_output, outbound_to_event, SidecarHandle lifecycle, wait_ready, send/abort/clear, from_parts_with_mcp MCP interception, spawn_sidecar_impl, and shutdown_sidecar. Many tests construct state with tokio::sync::Mutex and will need updating for S2. The from_parts_with_mcp test creates an EngineSession and will need updating for S1.

steps.description: S5: Change MSG_COUNTER fetch_add from Ordering::SeqCst to Ordering::Relaxed in send_message (line 381 of claude_bridge.rs). No behavior change — existing send_message_ids_are_unique_across_calls test covers correctness. SeqCst is unnecessarily strong for a monotonic counter with no cross-variable ordering requirements.

S4: Write test outbound_to_event_returns_static_str that explicitly type-annotates the return as (&str, Value). This fails to compile with the current (String, Value) return type because String cannot coerce to &str in destructuring. Also write test event_sink_accepts_str_ref that creates a from_parts_with_mcp handle with event_sink typed as Fn(&str, Value) — fails because current bound is Fn(String, Value).

S4: Change outbound_to_event return type from (String, Value) to (&'static str, Value) — all 7 match arms already use string literals which are &'static str. Update event_sink generic bound F from Fn(String, Value) to Fn(&str, Value) in from_parts_with_mcp, new_inner, and spawn_sidecar_impl. Update main.rs closure in claude_send_message (line 323: emit(&name, ...) works unchanged since &str satisfies Tauri's emit). Update test from_parts_with_mcp_intercepts_reify_tool_calls closure signature (name: String → name: &str). Update spawn_sidecar_impl test closures (_name: String → _name: &str).

S2: Write test from_parts_accepts_std_sync_mutex that constructs state as Arc<std::sync::Mutex<SidecarState>> and passes it to SidecarHandle::from_parts. Also write test state_accessor_returns_std_sync_mutex that calls handle.state().lock().unwrap() (synchronous). Both fail to compile because from_parts currently expects Arc<tokio::sync::Mutex<SidecarState>> and state() returns &Arc<tokio::sync::Mutex<...>>.

S2: Switch SidecarHandle.state from Arc<tokio::sync::Mutex<SidecarState>> to Arc<std::sync::Mutex<SidecarState>>. (1) Update the SidecarHandle struct field type. (2) Update state() accessor return type. (3) In new_inner on_message callback: replace tokio::spawn(async { state.lock().await = Ready; notify }) with direct state.lock().unwrap() = Ready + notify_waiters(). (4) In on_exit callback: same pattern — direct lock().unwrap() instead of tokio::spawn. (5) In wait_ready: change 3 occurrences of self.state.lock().await to self.state.lock().unwrap(). (6) In kill: change self.state.lock().await to self.state.lock().unwrap(). (7) In claude_send_message_impl: change handle.state().lock().await to handle.state().lock().unwrap(). (8) In spawn_sidecar_impl: change Arc::new(Mutex::new(...)) to Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(...)). (9) Remove tokio::sync::Mutex from imports (keep tokio::sync::Notify). (10) Update ~15 tests that create state with tokio::sync::Mutex to use std::sync::Mutex, and change .lock().await to .lock().unwrap().

S1: Write test from_parts_with_tool_dispatch_intercepts_reify_calls that creates a SidecarHandle using from_parts_with_mcp with a tool_dispatch closure (|tool_name: String, input: Value| -> Result<Value, String>) instead of an Arc<Mutex<EngineSession>>. The closure returns Ok(json!({"dispatched": true})). Simulates a reify_ tool_call from stdout, verifies the tool_result written back to stdin contains the dispatched result. This fails because current from_parts_with_mcp requires engine: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<EngineSession>>.

S1: Replace engine parameter with generic tool_dispatch callback. (1) In from_parts_with_mcp: replace engine: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<crate::engine::EngineSession>> with tool_dispatch: D where D: Fn(String, Value) -> Result<Value, String> + Send + Sync + 'static. (2) In new_inner: replace mcp_config tuple type from (Arc<...EngineSession>, F) to (D, F). (3) In the MCP interception branch: replace TauriToolContext::new + mcp_tool_call_impl with direct tool_dispatch(tool_name, tool_input) call. Remove crate::mcp_context and crate::engine imports from claude_bridge.rs. (4) In spawn_sidecar_impl: replace engine param with tool_dispatch, forward to from_parts_with_mcp. (5) In main.rs claude_send_message: construct TauriToolContext in the tool_dispatch closure passed to spawn_sidecar_impl, moving the engine dependency to the call site. (6) Update existing from_parts_with_mcp_intercepts_reify_tool_calls test to pass a tool_dispatch closure instead of engine. (7) Update spawn_sidecar_impl tests to pass tool_dispatch instead of engine.

S3: Write test subscribe_ready_returns_awaitable_future that calls handle.subscribe_ready(), stores the returned future, moves the handle into an Option slot, drops the reference, then awaits the future with a ready message. This verifies subscribe_ready produces an owned 'static future safe to hold across lock boundaries. Fails because subscribe_ready() does not exist yet.

S3: Add pub fn subscribe_ready(&self) -> impl Future<Output = ()> + Send + 'static that clones Arc<Notify> internally and returns async move { notify.notified().await }. Remove pub fn ready_notify() accessor entirely (it was only used in main.rs and internally). Internal uses in wait_ready already access self.ready_notify directly. Update main.rs claude_send_message to use handle.subscribe_ready() instead of Arc::clone(handle.ready_notify()).notified(). Verify test that used ready_notify() is updated or removed.

S8: Extract private async fn with_handle<T>(sidecar, f) helper using for<'a> FnOnce(&'a mut SidecarHandle) -> Pin<Box<dyn Future<Output = Result<T, String>> + Send + 'a>> for the closure type. The helper locks the outer sidecar Mutex, checks for None (returns Err 'sidecar not started'), and calls f(handle).await for the Some case. Refactor claude_abort_impl to with_handle(sidecar, |h| Box::pin(h.abort())). Refactor claude_clear_session_impl similarly. Refactor claude_send_message_impl: own text via to_string(), use with_handle with move closure that checks state (lock().unwrap()) and calls send_message. Existing tests for all three _impl functions validate behavior is preserved.

design_decisions.decision: tool_dispatch callback is synchronous: Fn(String, Value) -> Result<Value, String>
design_decisions.rationale: mcp_tool_call_impl is synchronous (locks engine mutex, runs tool, returns). The tokio::spawn in the MCP interception branch is needed for the async stdin write, not for tool dispatch itself. Keeping tool_dispatch sync avoids the complexity of async closures while matching the actual calling pattern.

design_decisions.decision: subscribe_ready() returns impl Future + 'static instead of exposing Arc<Notify>
design_decisions.rationale: Hides the Notify implementation detail while providing the same capability: an owned future that can be held across lock boundaries. main.rs needs this pattern to avoid holding the outer sidecar lock during the 10s ready timeout. The returned future clones the Arc internally so it's self-contained.

design_decisions.decision: with_handle uses Pin<Box<dyn Future + 'a>> for the closure return type
design_decisions.rationale: Rust's type system requires explicit lifetime propagation for async closures that borrow from their arguments. The for<'a> FnOnce(&'a mut SidecarHandle) -> Pin<Box<dyn Future + Send + 'a>> pattern is the standard workaround. The Box allocation is negligible compared to the outer async Mutex lock + sidecar IPC overhead.

design_decisions.decision: claude_send_message_impl owns text via to_string() when using with_handle
design_decisions.rationale: The for<'a> bound on with_handle's closure means captured references can't have a specific lifetime. Owning text (one small allocation per user message) avoids lifetime gymnastics. This is not a hot path — it runs once per user message.

design_decisions.decision: Step ordering: S5 → S4 → S2 → S1 → S3 → S8
design_decisions.rationale: S5 is independent (1 line). S4 changes event_sink type before S1 changes the same signature. S2 changes state mutex type before S1 uses it in closures. S3 depends on subscribe_ready alternative before removing ready_notify. S8 depends on S2 (uses lock().unwrap() in extracted helper). Each step touches an overlapping but distinct concern, so this ordering minimizes re-editing.

design_decisions.decision: on_message Ready transition uses direct lock().unwrap() instead of tokio::spawn
design_decisions.rationale: The on_message callback runs inside read_sidecar_output's tokio task. The std::sync::Mutex state lock is held for only a single assignment — no risk of blocking the async runtime. The tokio::spawn was unnecessary overhead that also introduced a race window where state might not be updated when expected.

reuse.how: from_parts is the test-friendly constructor that creates handles without MCP wiring. All test helpers that don't need MCP continue using it. Its generic type params for new_inner's None case need updating for the new D type parameter.

All existing tests use tokio::io::duplex for stdin/stdout simulation and BufReader for the reader. New tests follow the same pattern. The MCP interception test pattern (write tool_call to stdout_writer, read tool_result from stdin_reader) is reused for the S1 test.
After S1, these are called from main.rs inside the tool_dispatch closure rather than from claude_bridge.rs. The API is unchanged — only the call site moves.

reuse.what: Arc<Notify> pattern for async readiness signaling
reuse.how: The Notify + notified() pattern is preserved internally. subscribe_ready wraps it in a cleaner public API that returns an owned future.
